(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Woke up to that soft, relentless rain and immediately did the usual—coffee, quick tidy, then a glance at the commute like it’s a weather forecast for my mood. SR-520 being closed eastbound from 9 to noon definitely turned my morning into a little game of “what route feels least awful,” so I left a bit earlier and hugged the extra time buffer. At QFC in Woodinville I grabbed breakfast-to-go under my umbrella, feeling mildly damp but functional. Work in Sammamish felt cozy anyway: I polished a couple UI bits, ran build tests, and then got pulled into a design review where my brain stayed on quest layout and accessibility tweaks. The rain eased my energy, but not my focus. Later, Café Allegro’s warmth and my notes kept me steady. I ended the day at The Ram by Pike Place Market with a quiet dinner and then came home to a record—jotted birdwatching ideas, listened to the rain fade into silence. Honestly, it felt like a good reset.
thought_process: I planned my timing around the SR-520 closure because I don’t want stress to leak into work; when traffic decides to misbehave, my brain gets less creative and more reactive. The steady drizzle also made me choose indoor errands carefully—Safeway for supplies, then a quick REI stop for a transit map and a snack—so I could stay moving without getting soaked again. At work, I focused on UI polish and quest accessibility because those are “small changes with clear feedback,” which felt especially satisfying on a grey day. The mid-morning design review nudged me to reconsider layout details instead of pushing through assumptions. By the time I reached Café Allegro and later The Ram, my mood had warmed up—less about the weather, more about feeling useful and connected. I ended calmer than I started.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
